message:
perf(transport): replace Mutex timestamps with AtomicU64 and migrate BTreeMap to HashMap in aggregator and router

Transport aggregator:
- TransportPath.last_activity: RwLock<Time> → AtomicU64 (nanoseconds). The
  record_receipt() hot path now does a single atomic store instead of acquiring
  a write lock.
- MultipathAggregator.last_flush: RwLock<Time> → AtomicU64 with CAS loop.
  flush() uses compare_exchange_weak to atomically check the interval and
  update the timestamp, eliminating the write lock entirely.
- PathSet, SymbolDeduplicator, SymbolReorderer, ObjectDeduplicationState:
  all internal BTreeMap/BTreeSet replaced with HashMap/HashSet since iteration
  order is irrelevant for deduplication and reordering logic.

Transport router:
- Endpoint.last_success and last_failure: RwLock<Option<Time>> → AtomicU64
  with 0 = None convention. record_success()/record_failure() now do a single
  atomic store.
- Endpoint.metadata: BTreeMap → HashMap.
- RoutingTable routes and endpoints maps: BTreeMap → HashMap.
- SymbolDispatcher sinks map: BTreeMap → HashMap.
- EndpointSinkMap type alias updated accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
